name: Turkey Pastrami
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, combine water, sugar, and salt. Bring to a boil, stirring until solids dissolve.
2. Remove from heat and add dry spice mixture. Let cool.
3. Pour the cooled brine mixture over the turkey breast, ensuring it is completely covered. Refrigerate, covered, for 48 hours.
4. Preheat oven to 250 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. Remove turkey from brine and rinse under cold water. Dry thoroughly with paper towels using the palms of your hands.
6. Press approximately two-thirds of the dry rub mixture onto the skin side of the breast.
7. Press the remaining mixture onto the other side.
8. Place the breast on a rack, skin-side down, and bake for 1 1/2 hours.
9. Cool and wrap tightly in plastic. The pastrami will improve in flavor if aged for up to one week.
Yield: 2 1/2 pound Turkey Breast
